title: Item Views vs Purchases - SQL Join and Count

content.problemStatement:
We want to focus on the customers journey. \
For each customer who visited the website, we want to know how many products did he view across sessions and how many products did he buy across sessions?

### Task
Write a query to do the following
- Output user_id, count_viewed, count_purchased
- You need to compute the following
   - Total items viewed by each user_id as 'count_viewed'
   - Total items purchased by each user_id as 'count_purchased'
```
Expected output
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ user_id в”Ӯ count_viewed в”Ӯ count_purchased в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ 1       в”Ӯ 22           в”Ӯ 22              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 2       в”Ӯ 22           в”Ӯ 0               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 4       в”Ӯ 11           в”Ӯ 0               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 5       в”Ӯ 11           в”Ӯ 11              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 6       в”Ӯ 11           в”Ӯ 0               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 7       в”Ӯ 11           в”Ӯ 11              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 9       в”Ӯ 22           в”Ӯ 11              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 10      в”Ӯ 20           в”Ӯ 9               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 15      в”Ӯ 11           в”Ӯ 0               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 18      в”Ӯ 22           в”Ӯ 0               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 19      в”Ӯ 11           в”Ӯ 0               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 21      в”Ӯ 22           в”Ӯ 0               в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```
### Table formats
1. webpage_visits
- session_id
- user_id
- item_viewed

2. add_to_cart
- session_id
- user_id
- item_added

3. purchase
- session_id
- user_id
- item_purchased
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ вҖ” the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
